title: Apple's $100 billion investment sparks rally and hints at a tariff carveout playbook
body: Apple announced a massive $100 billion US investment plan, and markets treated it like both a patriotic headline and a potential roadmap for winning tariff relief. Bulls read it as industrial policy meeting corporate strategy. Critics read it as a costly political toll booth. The story mixes capital spending, election-era signaling, and the idea that the biggest companies can buy certainty while everyone else pays retail.
image_descriptions: A polished Apple logo displayed during a corporate event, signaling scale, prestige, and message discipline.
A market-themed hero image implying investor excitement rather than factory-floor detail.
